content: @Miguel Ojeda I see that 0-day CI is building all the Rust samples. Do you know if they also build the doctests? If not, we might want to ask them to build it.

I thought about this because I asked for examples for @Alice Ryhl workqueue patches. I implicitly meant samples in samples/rust/* but Alice created examples inline in the documentation, which super cool , since they are rendered in the documentation.

Andreas Hindborg said:

Miguel Ojeda I see that 0-day CI is building all the Rust samples. Do you know if they also build the doctests? If not, we might want to ask them to build it.

I don't think they are building them, but I have asked them. Also about possibly running make rustdoc too.

Andreas Hindborg said:

I thought about this because I asked for examples for Alice Ryhl workqueue patches. I implicitly meant samples in samples/rust/* but Alice created examples inline in the documentation, which super cool , since they are rendered in the documentation.

Indeed! I think it is great to have docs that double as tests. I am glad you like it :)

Miguel Ojeda said:

I don't think they are building them, but I have asked them. Also about possibly running make rustdoc too.

Good news -- they said they will take a look soon into supporting both features.
